apply: Explain the factors affecting transpiration in plants
Describe the structure and function of stomata in plants
Apply the knowledge of transport in plants to explain the movement of substances in plants
analyze: Analyze the importance of transport in plants for their survival
Compare and contrast the types of transport in plants
Evaluate the role of transport in plants in relation to their environment
remember: Recall the types of transport in plants (transpiration, translocation, and guttation)
Identify the main components of xylem and phloem tissues
List the functions of xylem and phloem in plants
understand: Explain the process of transpiration and its importance in plants
Describe the mechanism of translocation of sugars and other organic compounds in plants
Discuss the role of guttation in plants

ncert_objectives: Describe the types of transport in plants and their importance.
Explain the mechanism of translocation of sugars and other organic compounds in plants.
Discuss the factors affecting transpiration in plants and their role in plant growth and development.
board_exam_objectives: CBSE tests the types of transport in plants, transpiration, and translocation in its board exams.
CBSE assesses the student's understanding of the factors affecting transpiration, the structure and function of stomata, and the role of guttation in plants.
CBSE evaluates the student's ability to apply the knowledge of transport in plants to explain the movement of substances in plants and their importance in plant growth and development.
